description: Canonical error codes emitted by the idempotency/v1 A2A extension. Codes are surfaced in JSON-RPC error.data.code as stable string identifiers; the numeric error.code mirrors the HTTP status where reasonable.

errors.description: The submitted Message.message_id matches a cached request within TTL, but the canonicalized request body (JCS RFC 8785, SHA-256) does not match the cached entry. The cached entry is preserved; this collision attempt MUST NOT overwrite it. Clients MUST either retry with the original payload (which will return the cached response with Idempotent-Replayed: true) or generate a fresh Message.message_id for a genuinely new request.

data_fields.original_request_hash: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the canonicalized body that was cached.
data_fields.submitted_request_hash: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the canonicalized body submitted in the colliding request.

errors.description: Message.message_id is missing, not a string, or fails UUID-shape validation. The extension requires UUIDv7 or a 128-bit cryptographically random value rendered as a canonical UUID string. Lower-entropy schemes (incrementing integers, low-bit timestamps, etc.) are rejected to prevent collision-induced false-positive deduplication or false-positive 422 collisions against unrelated clients.

data_fields.message_id: The submitted value (or null if absent), echoed for diagnostic purposes.

errors.description: The server advertises idempotency/v1 with required: true, but the request did not include the extension URI in the A2A-Extensions activation header. Per A2A core, required extensions MUST be activated by the client; in-scope mutating requests without the activation header are rejected before any side effects occur.

data_fields.missing_extension: The extension URI that was required but not activated.
